readinessNote: This exhausts the complete high-recall HKCanCor preceding-v/v1/xv + 咗 + following-r comparison track. It supplies classified attestation and boundary evidence but cannot establish productivity, complete lexical scope, corpus readiness, status, or promotion.

claimCrosswalk.corpusQuestion: Does HKCanCor naturally attest lexical V + perfective 咗 + an overt pronoun, demonstrative, or other r-tagged NP onset in this bounded query?
Does every token tagged v, v1, or xv + 咗 + an r-tagged token instantiate the narrow overt-object profile?
Can this complete high-recall POS slice settle unrestricted predicate-object productivity or all temporal, completion, boundedness, and current-relevance analyses?

decisions.reviewerNote: 變咗 functions as a discourse/result transition; 佢 begins the following clause or topic phrase rather than serving as an overt object of 變.
decisions.exclusionReason: Following pronoun is not the object licensed by the narrow AB30 profile.

decisions.reviewerNote: The surrounding money-and-goods exchange makes 佢 the overt recipient argument after 俾咗.
佢 refers to the goods under discussion and is the overt object of 要咗.
佢 refers to the exchanged item and is the overt object of 封咗; 收埋佢 confirms the same referent.
The imperative contains a transparent 摺 + 咗 + overt pronominal object.
The banknote under discussion is resumed by 佢 as the overt object of 賣咗.
The preceding dead-turtle discussion supplies the referent, and 佢 is the overt object of 飲咗.
The complete post-marker object is the overt possessive NP 佢個頭, not merely the adjacent pronoun.
The prior translation-job offer supplies the referent rejected by 推咗佢.
The same job-offer discourse makes 佢 the overt object of 推咗.
佢 resumes 成個缸 and is the overt object of 掉咗.
The preceding stranger asking questions identifies 佢 as the overt addressee object of 答咗.

The camera discussion suggests a transitive action with 佢 as its object, but the romanized predicate pit3 is not recoverable precisely enough from the transcript to assign a secure construction analysis.
decisions.exclusionReason: Opaque romanized predicate prevents a secure lexical and syntactic classification.

decisions.reviewerNote: 佢 is the overt person object of 叫咗 and precedes the directional continuation 出門口嗰度.
The relevant complement is the proposition 佢壞 ('it breaks'); 佢 is the subject of the following predicate, not an overt NP object selected by 預咗.
decisions.exclusionReason: Following pronoun begins a clausal complement rather than the narrow AB30 object.

decisions.reviewerNote: In 賺咗佢一二千蚊, 佢 may be a referential object or a colloquial dummy before the amount expression; the available turn does not resolve that role.
decisions.exclusionReason: The pronominal role cannot be distinguished confidently from a dummy-plus-amount profile.

decisions.reviewerNote: 佢 is the overt object recorded before the recording is shown to others.
The legal-enforcement context makes 佢 the overt person object of 拉咗 ('arrested him/her').
The complete overt object is the headless relative NP 佢應該要賺嘅嘢 ('what he should earn'), which begins immediately after 咗.
The suicidal person is resumed by 佢 as the overt object of 幫咗.
The complete overt object is the possessive NP 佢舖 ('his shop'), following 封咗.
The complete overt object is the possessive NP 佢嘅行為 ('his behavior'), following 決定咗.
The previous turn identifies 哩個話題, which 佢 resumes as the overt object of 結束咗.
佢自己本身 is an overt reflexive-emphatic NP object of 講咗; the following 作為 phrase elaborates that referent.
The quoted threat contains a transparent 閹 + 咗 + overt person object.
The second quoted threat independently contains a transparent 閹 + 咗 + overt person object.
The complete overt object is the possessive NP 佢嘅工作 ('his/her work'), following 分擔咗.
你 begins the consequence clause 你可以睇嘢 after discourse-result 變咗; it is not an object of 變.
decisions.exclusionReason: Following pronoun is the subject of a new clause.

decisions.reviewerNote: 你 is the subject of the following clause 你唔使坐咁耐機 after discourse-result 變咗.
decisions.exclusionReason: Following pronoun is the subject of a new clause.

decisions.reviewerNote: 變咗 introduces a result/discourse transition and 你 starts the following repaired phrase, rather than serving as an object.
decisions.exclusionReason: Following pronoun is not an overt object of 變.

decisions.reviewerNote: 我 is the subject of 我而家有啲郵票 after resultative discourse 變咗.
decisions.exclusionReason: Following pronoun is the subject of a new clause.

decisions.reviewerNote: The full demonstrative object 嗰張 is the stamp bought and taken home.
你 is the subject of 你真係永遠就自己揸住來睇 after discourse-result 變咗.
decisions.exclusionReason: Following pronoun is the subject of a new clause.

decisions.reviewerNote: In the running context, 我 is the overt object of 過咗 in the sense of overtaking the speaker.
第二啲路 is a path complement of the motion predicate 走咗, not the licensed overt object targeted by AB30.
decisions.exclusionReason: Motion-path complement falls outside the narrow overt-object profile.

decisions.reviewerNote: 你個鼻嗰度 supplies the goal/location reached by dog hair flying, not an object of 飛咗.
